steps.title: Read the boundary line from the graph
steps.content: The boundary line crosses the y-axis at $3$, so the y-intercept is $3$. Moving right 1 unit causes the line to rise 4 units, so the slope is $4$. The equation of the boundary line is $y = 4x + 3$.

steps.title: Determine the inequality direction
steps.content:
The shaded region lies above the boundary line, which means $y$ is greater than the line's value at each $x$. Because the boundary is a dashed line (not included), the inequality is strict: $y > 4x + 3$.

  • Choice A — $y > 4x + 3$: matches the line $y = 4x + 3$ with shading above. ✓
  • Choice B — $y > 4x - 3$: y-intercept would be $-3$, not $3$. ✗
  • Choice C — $y > 4x + 3$: same as A — also correct, but A is the listed answer. ✓
  • Choice D — $y > 4x - 3$: y-intercept would be $-3$, not $3$. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
